channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Catholic community in Utah 'united in prayer' for the health of Pope Francis
item.description: The pope showed some slight improvements in recent tests, and as he continues to recover, members of the Catholic faith around the world — including in Utah — continue to pray for his health.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Anti-ICE activists disrupt LA operations, post photos, names and phone numbers of agents
item.description: A group of anti-ICE activists interrupted ICE operations in Southern California on Monday and terrorized agents by posting flyers with ICE agents names, images, phone numbers and addresses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In Rome, talks to protect Earth's biodiversity resume with money topping the agenda
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — An annual United Nations conference on biodiversity that ran out of time last year will resume its work Tuesday in Rome with money at the top of the agenda.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 46.1 million people were counted in Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years
item.description: BAGHDAD (AP) — The final results of Iraq's first census in nearly 40 years released Monday show the population has reached 46.1 million. In 2009, an unofficial count estimated the population at 31.6 million.
item.dc:creator: QASSIM ABDUL-ZAHRA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Hegseth says he fired the top military lawyers because they weren't well suited for the jobs
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Monday that he was replacing the top lawyers for the military services because he didn't think they were “well-suited” to provide recommendations when lawful orders are given.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Mississippi city drops lawsuit over newspaper editorial that judge ordered removed
item.description: A Mississippi city is dropping its lawsuit against a newspaper that had its editorial criticizing local officials removed by a judge.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Demillo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Antivirus software company ordered to pay customer $16.5M: How to file a claim
item.description: Millions of American consumers who bought antivirus software from Avast may be eligible to receive a portion of a $16.5 million settlement, the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Trump expresses hope Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he meets with France's Macron
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump expressed hope that Russia's war in Ukraine is nearing an endgame as he met Monday with French President Emmanuel Macron on the third anniversary of the invasion.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET, MATTHEW LEE and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Under threat of termination, VA workers return to the office
item.description: President Donald Trump has ordered federal workers to return to the office, and that’s exactly what happened at the Department of Veterans Affairs on Monday. “This is an exciting day,” VA Secretary Doug Collins said in a video posted on social media that included footage of him greeting employees.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: The FBI's new deputy director is a popular podcaster
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The popular right-wing podcaster Dan Bongino has built a career of unleashing sometimes inflammatory rants against the media, Democrats and the federal government. Now, the 50-year-old former New York police officer and U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: McConnell suggests Trump has ‘gross misunderstanding’ of Ukraine talks
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (R-Ky.) on Monday suggested that President Trump’s refusal to blame Russia as the sole aggressor who provoked the war in Ukraine “reflects a gross misunderstanding of the nature of negotiations and leverage.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minimum wage workers would have to work 106 hours to afford average apartment, report finds
item.description: A new report found that a minimum-wage employee would have to work 106 hours a week to afford a median-priced apartment in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Angry Democratic donors turn off the flow of money
item.description: Democrats are anxious to rebuild their party on the heels of President Donald Trump’s victory in November. But they have a major problem as they try to refashion their brand: The money isn’t there.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia-Ukraine war enters third year
item.description: As the war in Ukraine enters its third year, the United States is ramping up efforts to broker a peace deal, but sharp divisions are emerging over how to approach Russia’s role in the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Reshad Hudson, KRON4

item.title: Trump posts SpongeBob meme mocking federal workers
item.description: Trump posted a SpongeBob meme mocking federal employees in the wake of an email sent asking them to document their achievements.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: How to keep your kids safe online
item.description: Screens are a part of life and kids are on them more than ever. "Children are spending up upwards of 8 hours a day on connected tech," started Titania Jordan, Chief Parent Officer at Bark Technologies, a platform that lets parents monitor, filter and limit what their kids do online.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Come 'Beyond the Gates' for the first Black-led soap opera
item.description: The first thing viewers of “Beyond the Gates” see is a black Mercedes gliding past manicured lawns and stately estates.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 11 dead after supplemental shakes linked to listeria cases in 21 states, including Florida
item.description: Frozen shakes sold to nursing homes and other institutions are being recalled after a listeria outbreak in 21 states that has caused 11 deaths, according to the Federal Food and Drug Administration.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Johnston, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Possible motive identified for Pennsylvania hospital shooting
item.description: The man who entered the hospital ICU armed with a semi-automatic handgun and zip ties was "upset" over the "lack of care" for a family member, police said.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Eric Adams says NYC's Roosevelt Hotel migrant shelter will soon close
item.description: The massive Roosevelt Hotel migrant shelter in Manhattan will shut down in the next few months, New York City Mayor Eric Adams announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces Dan Bongino as Deputy FBI Director
item.description: Bongino is a former U.S. Secret Service agent that became known for his defense of Trump while previously appearing as a Fox News contributor.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal workers confront mass confusion as Musk's deadline looms
item.description: Confusion and chaos loom as hundreds of thousands of federal employees begin their workweek facing a deadline from Elon Musk to explain their recent accomplishments or risk losing their jobs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jury selection begins for trial of suburban Chicago July 4 parade mass shooting suspect
item.description: Jury selection is underway for the trial of the man accused of opening fire on a suburban Chicago Independence Day parade and killing seven people in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US Coast Guard rescues 3 after boat capsizes off Florida coast: video
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard shared footage from a weekend rescue after three overdue boaters were stranded off the coast of Florida when their boat capsized.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ohio bathroom law targeting transgender students has brought internal strife to some campuses
item.description: For some famously progressive colleges in Ohio, a new state law designed to keep transgender women from using women’s restrooms at schools is bringing a moment of soul-searching for students, alumni and administrators.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Illegal Brazilian gang member convicted of assault flashes sinister smile during arrest in Massachusetts
item.description: An illegal immigrant convicted of assault was caught smiling while being apprehended in an immigration enforcement operation in Massachusetts earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How possible USPS changes could affect your mail
item.description: “We want to have a post office that works well and doesn’t lose massive amounts of money," Trump said. “We’re thinking about doing that. And it’ll be a form of a merger, but it’ll remain the Postal Service, and I think it’ll operate a lot better.
item.dc:creator: Brad Hamilton, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, Musk aren't the first to make deep cuts: Clinton-era initiative saved billions
item.description: DOGE isn't the first controversial cost-cutting push — the biggest effort to overhaul the federal government in modern history actually was 30 years ago under a Democratic administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luxury portable toilets sought after ahead of NFL Draft
item.description: The luxury restroom trailers and containers feature flushing toilets, sinks, air conditioning, and even stereo systems.
item.dc:creator: Timothy Littau, WGN 9

item.title: Lynne Marie Stewart of 'Pee-wee's Playhouse,' 'It's Always Sunny' dies: reports
item.description: The actress has recently finished filming for another season of "It's Always Sunny in Philadelphia" and her forthcoming pickleball-related film, "Dink.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Pope Francis remains in critical condition with early kidney failure
item.description: Pope Francis was conscious but still receiving high flows of supplemental oxygen Sunday following a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, as he remains in critical condition with a complex lung infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison for murder he denied committing, celebrates with steak dinner
item.description: A Hawaii man was freed after spending 30 years in prison for a murder he says he did not commit, and one of the first things he did after his release was visit his mother's grave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Watch live: Trump set to address CPAC
item.description: President Trump on Saturday afternoon will headline the final day of the Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) in Maryland.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, Fox 5

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: National Park Service restores some jobs of fired employees, pledges to hire 7,700 seasonal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is restoring jobs for dozens of National Park Service employees fired amid government-wide reductions and hiring nearly 3,000 additional seasonal workers, following an uproar over an aggressive plan to downsize the agency.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4
